title: KlaipГ„вЂ”da Port to Begin Construction of LithuaniaГўв‚¬в„ўs First Green Hydrogen Hub
content: The Port of KlaipГѓВ©da in Lithuania will begin construction of a green hydrogen production and refuelling facility suitable for maritime and transport operations. The KlaipГ„вЂ”da State Seaport Authority has received a construction permit and is expected to begin work this month, with hydrogen production slated to start in 2026. The ГўвЂљВ¬12m ($13.7m) project is part of EuropeГўв‚¬в„ўs Next Generation Lithuania recovery plan, with ГўвЂљВ¬6m ($6.8m) co-funded by the EUГўв‚¬в„ўs NextGenerationEU facility. The project will require users to comply with the EUГўв‚¬в„ўs FuelEU Maritime regulation, which promotes green hydrogen use in shipping and sets renewable fuels of non-biological origin (RFNBO) targets of 1% by 2031 and 2% by 2034.
author: Analysts Account
